## [15:10 - 18:11]

it could be a share, or it could be a purchase. Then there's another one that's called social proof. And this is the idea. And in here, authority is super powerful early on in your sideways sales letter. Reciprocity comes all through here. Social proof comes all through here because usually in these videos, you'll ask for comments, you'll ask for reactions. And often on your pre-launch pages, there'll be a place to put in comments down below. And when all of a sudden there's comments down there, people can see that other people are engaging, they can see people are interested, they can see the social proof of you just being an authority. And that's very, very powerful. So it's this idea of if we see other people doing something, then we think there's something there. This is why when you go to Amazon and you see there's a whole bunch of reviews on a product, that's social proof, especially if there are a whole bunch of positive reviews. But even if there's just a lot of reviews, it's like, okay, well, a lot of people are looking at this. Then there's another one called community. And we act in response to the way we think our community is supposed to act. And if you have, you can actually build up a community just in the comment section below these videos.
